title: Amenities & features catalog by vertical (scoping + i18n source-of-truth)

tasks.title: Add applicableVerticals column + drop name in one structural migration
tasks.description:
Generate a single Drizzle structural migration that does ALL of the following in one SQL file (both tables, both changes), so amenities/features are never migrated twice:

- ALTER TABLE amenities ADD COLUMN applicable_verticals text[] NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}';
- ALTER TABLE features ADD COLUMN applicable_verticals text[] NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}';
- ALTER TABLE amenities DROP COLUMN name;
- ALTER TABLE features DROP COLUMN name;

Files affected:
- `packages/db/src/schemas/accommodation/amenity.dbschema.ts` — add `applicableVerticals: text('applicable_verticals').array().notNull().default([])`, remove `name` column.
- `packages/db/src/schemas/accommodation/feature.dbschema.ts` — same.
- Run `pnpm db:generate` to produce the migration, then hand-inspect it to confirm both DDL statements are in the same file (Drizzle may split them). If split, merge into a single file and re-validate.
- The migration must be committed to `packages/db/src/migrations/` (the structural carril).

Acceptance criteria:
- `pnpm db:generate` + `pnpm db:migrate` both succeed locally with no errors.
- `pnpm --filter @repo/db typecheck` is green.
- `packages/db/src/migrations/` contains exactly ONE new `.sql` file that drops `name` and adds `applicable_verticals` on both tables.
- No extras carril entry needed (no triggers/views/check constraints).

Testing: run `pnpm --filter @repo/db test` (existing schema/model tests must remain green after the column rename/drop; if any test references `name`, update it in this task).

subtasks.title: Update amenity.dbschema.ts: remove name, add applicableVerticals
Update feature.dbschema.ts: remove name, add applicableVerticals
Run pnpm db:generate and verify single combined migration file
Fix any DB model/test references to the name column

tasks.title: Update Zod schemas in @repo/schemas for amenity and feature
tasks.description:
Update the Zod schemas that validate amenity/feature catalog items to reflect the structural changes from T-001:

- Remove the `name` field (JsonbI18nSchema or similar) from `AmenitySchema`, `AmenityCreateInputSchema`, `AmenityUpdateInputSchema`, and their feature equivalents.
- Add `applicableVerticals: z.array(z.enum(['accommodation','gastronomy','experience'])).min(1)` to both insert/update schemas. The runtime schema (for SELECT responses) also includes `applicableVerticals`.
- Relax the `slug` Zod regex from the current hyphen-only pattern to allow underscores: `^[a-z0-9]+(?:[-_][a-z0-9]+)*$` (Decision §5.3). Update the regex in both amenity and feature slug validators.
- Add `applicableVerticals` to all list/filter query schemas (e.g. `AmenityListQuerySchema`) as an optional filter: `z.enum(['accommodation','gastronomy','experience']).optional()`.

Files affected:
- `packages/schemas/src/accommodation/amenity.schema.ts` (or wherever the amenity schemas live — verify the actual path first)
- `packages/schemas/src/accommodation/feature.schema.ts`

Acceptance criteria:
- `pnpm --filter @repo/schemas typecheck` is green.
- `pnpm --filter @repo/schemas test` is green (existing tests pass; add new tests for the slug regex allowing `air_conditioning` and rejecting `Air_conditioning`, `air conditioning`, `air--conditioning`).
- The `AmenitySelectSchema` / `FeatureSelectSchema` no longer contain a `name` field.
- The `applicableVerticals` filter in list query schemas is optional and does not break existing callers.

Testing requirements: update slug regex tests; add vertical filter param tests.

subtasks.title: Remove name field from amenity and feature Zod schemas
Add applicableVerticals field to schemas and list query schemas
Relax slug regex to allow underscores in both schema files
Update/add unit tests for slug regex + applicableVerticals validation

tasks.title: Create featureNames i18n namespace + migrate amenity/feature display resolution everywhere
tasks.description:
Absorbs BETA-90 i18n half. Three sub-concerns in one task (they are tightly coupled and must not be split or the system will be inconsistent mid-task):

**A. Create featureNames namespace (es/en/pt)**
Add `featureNames` to `@repo/i18n` in all three locales, mirroring the existing `amenityNames` namespace. Keys are the feature slugs (snake_case). Seed the initial keys for the existing accommodation features (read current seed data to get all slugs).

Files affected:
- `packages/i18n/src/locales/es/features.ts` (new or appended to existing i18n file)
- `packages/i18n/src/locales/en/features.ts`
- `packages/i18n/src/locales/pt/features.ts`
- Export from the i18n package root.

**B. Update service search: name->>'es' -> slug**
The amenity/feature search in `@repo/service-core` currently filters `name->>'es'` (a JSONB extract). With `name` gone, search must switch to filtering on `slug`. Find the exact query location — likely `packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation/amenity.service.ts` (and feature equivalent) — and update the WHERE clause to use `ilike(amenity.slug, ...)` via `safeIlike()` (never raw `ilike()`).

**C. Remove catalog-names.ts shim (web)**
`apps/web/src/lib/catalog-names.ts` (the interim `translateAmenityName` / similar) reads from i18n with a humanized fallback because `featureNames` didn't exist. With both namespaces present, inline the i18n call at call sites and delete this file. Update all imports.

Acceptance criteria:
- `pnpm --filter @repo/i18n test` green (i18n completeness test must see all existing amenity/feature slugs covered in es/en/pt).
- `pnpm --filter @repo/service-core typecheck` green.
- `apps/web/src/lib/catalog-names.ts` no longer exists.
- `grep -r 'catalog-names' apps/web/src` returns nothing.
- `grep -r "name->>'es'" packages/service-core/src` returns nothing.
- `grep -rn 'ilike(' packages/service-core/src` returns nothing (safeIlike() only).

subtasks.title: Add featureNames namespace in es/en/pt with all existing feature slugs as keys
Update service-core search from name->>'es' to safeIlike on slug
Delete apps/web/src/lib/catalog-names.ts and update all its import sites
Verify i18n completeness test passes for both namespaces in all locales

tasks.title: Re-seed amenities and features with applicableVerticals + gastronomy/experience taxonomy
tasks.description:
Update `@repo/seed` to deliver the full owner-approved taxonomy from §10:

**A. Re-tag existing accommodation items**
Shared-core slugs get `applicableVerticals = ['accommodation','gastronomy','experience']`:
`wifi`, `parking`, `pet_friendly`, `wheelchair_accessible`, `air_conditioning`, `outdoor_seating`, `smoke_free`, `accepts_cards`.
All other existing accommodation amenities/features keep `applicableVerticals = ['accommodation']`.

**B. Author new gastronomy amenities (10)**
`delivery`, `takeaway`, `accepts_reservations`, `live_music`, `happy_hour`, `bike_parking`, `kids_play_area`, `private_events`, `catering`, `bar_service`
— all with `applicableVerticals = ['gastronomy']`.

**C. Author new gastronomy features (9)**
`gluten_free_options`, `vegan_options`, `vegetarian_options`, `kids_menu`, `panoramic_view`, `family_friendly`, `romantic_ambiance`, `organic_products`, `craft_beer`
— all with `applicableVerticals = ['gastronomy']`.

**D. Author new experience amenities (8)**
`transport_included`, `equipment_included`, `food_included`, `guide_included`, `photos_included`, `insurance_included`, `hotel_pickup`, `lockers_available`
— all with `applicableVerticals = ['experience']`.

**E. Author new experience features (8)**
`kid_friendly`, `beginner_friendly`, `english_guide_available`, `portuguese_guide_available`, `outdoor_activity`, `pregnancy_safe`, `private_available`, `senior_friendly`
— all with `applicableVerticals = ['experience']`.

The seed upserts by slug so existing data is updated, not duplicated.

Files affected:
- `packages/seed/src/accommodation/amenities.seed.ts` (or equivalent JSON seed files)
- `packages/seed/src/accommodation/features.seed.ts`

Acceptance criteria:
- `pnpm db:fresh-dev` runs without errors.
- After seeding: `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM amenities WHERE 'gastronomy' = ANY(applicable_verticals)` returns 18 (10 amenities + 8 shared-core).
- After seeding: `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM features WHERE 'experience' = ANY(applicable_verticals)` returns 16 (8 features + 8 shared-core).
- Existing accommodation relation rows (r_accommodation_amenity, r_accommodation_feature) are NOT affected by the seed update.

Testing: add a seed-smoke test that asserts the counts above against a test DB.

subtasks.title: Re-tag shared-core items with all 3 verticals in amenities seed
Add 10 gastronomy amenities + 9 gastronomy features to seed
Add 8 experience amenities + 8 experience features to seed
Add seed-smoke assertions for vertical counts

tasks.title: Add vertical filter to public catalog endpoints
tasks.description:
Update the two public catalog endpoints to accept and honor a `?vertical=` query parameter:

- `GET /api/v1/public/amenities` — add optional `vertical: z.enum(['accommodation','gastronomy','experience']).optional()` to the query schema. When present, filter: `WHERE 'vertical' = ANY(applicable_verticals)`. When absent, return the full catalog (backward-compatible).
- `GET /api/v1/public/features` — identical change.

Use the `applicableVerticals` filter already added to the Zod query schemas in T-002. The service layer (AmenityService / FeatureService) must accept and forward the filter to the model query.

Files affected:
- `apps/api/src/routes/public/amenities/` (route handler + query schema usage)
- `apps/api/src/routes/public/features/` (route handler + query schema usage)
- `packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation/amenity.service.ts` (list filter)
- `packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation/feature.service.ts` (list filter)
- `packages/db/src/models/accommodation/amenity.model.ts` (add vertical filter to WHERE)
- `packages/db/src/models/accommodation/feature.model.ts` (add vertical filter to WHERE)

Acceptance criteria:
- `GET /api/v1/public/amenities?vertical=gastronomy` returns only gastronomy + shared-core items (after T-004 is seeded).
- `GET /api/v1/public/amenities` (no filter) returns the full catalog.
- An invalid vertical value (`?vertical=invalid`) returns 422 with a Zod validation error.
- `pnpm --filter @repo/api typecheck` is green.

Testing: integration tests for the route covering: (1) no filter returns all, (2) `?vertical=accommodation` returns accommodation subset, (3) invalid vertical returns 422.

subtasks.title: Add vertical filter to AmenityModel and FeatureModel list queries
Forward vertical filter through service list() methods
Update public amenities and features route query schema to accept vertical param
Write integration tests: no-filter / vertical-filter / invalid-vertical

tasks.title: Update i18n keys for all new gastronomy and experience slugs
tasks.description:
Extend the `amenityNames` and `featureNames` i18n namespaces (created in T-003) to cover the new gastronomy and experience slugs seeded in T-004. This task is the i18n authoring for the new taxonomy — T-003 covered the existing catalog; T-006 covers the new entries.

**Amenity keys to add to amenityNames (es/en/pt):**
- Gastronomy (10): `delivery`, `takeaway`, `accepts_reservations`, `live_music`, `happy_hour`, `bike_parking`, `kids_play_area`, `private_events`, `catering`, `bar_service`
- Experience (8): `transport_included`, `equipment_included`, `food_included`, `guide_included`, `photos_included`, `insurance_included`, `hotel_pickup`, `lockers_available`

**Feature keys to add to featureNames (es/en/pt):**
- Gastronomy (9): `gluten_free_options`, `vegan_options`, `vegetarian_options`, `kids_menu`, `panoramic_view`, `family_friendly`, `romantic_ambiance`, `organic_products`, `craft_beer`
- Experience (8): `kid_friendly`, `beginner_friendly`, `english_guide_available`, `portuguese_guide_available`, `outdoor_activity`, `pregnancy_safe`, `private_available`, `senior_friendly`

All labels must be semantically correct in es/en/pt (not machine-translated). Spanish is the primary locale; English and Portuguese translations should be human-quality.

Files affected:
- `packages/i18n/src/locales/es/amenities.ts` (or wherever amenityNames lives)
- `packages/i18n/src/locales/en/amenities.ts`
- `packages/i18n/src/locales/pt/amenities.ts`
- Same for featureNames files.

Acceptance criteria:
- `pnpm --filter @repo/i18n test` green — the completeness test must find keys for every slug in both namespaces across all 3 locales.
- No placeholder/machine-translated strings for the primary `es` locale.

subtasks.title: Add 18 gastronomy amenity/feature i18n keys in es/en/pt
Add 16 experience amenity/feature i18n keys in es/en/pt
Verify i18n completeness test passes for all new slugs

tasks.title: Update accommodation editor (AmenitiesSection.client.tsx) to use vertical-filtered catalog + i18n labels
tasks.description:
Update the accommodation editor's amenities component to:
1. Pass `?vertical=accommodation` when fetching the catalog (so shared-core + accommodation-only items are returned and gastronomy/experience-only items are excluded).
2. Resolve display labels from `amenityNames.<slug>` via `@repo/i18n` instead of the now-deleted `catalog-names.ts` shim.
3. Also update the equivalent features component for the accommodation editor.

Files affected:
- `apps/web/src/components/host/editor/AmenitiesSection.client.tsx` — update the fetch call to include `?vertical=accommodation`; update label resolution.
- `apps/web/src/components/host/editor/` — any sibling features component.
- Verify the fetch URL; it may be a shared hook or a direct `fetch()` call.

Do NOT change what amenities/features the accommodation editor covers — regression risk. The filter just ensures the catalog matches the accommodation vertical. If the seeded `applicableVerticals = ['accommodation']` items already match the existing catalog exactly, the rendered set is unchanged.

Acceptance criteria:
- The accommodation editor renders the same amenity/feature set as before (no regression).
- Labels come from `@repo/i18n` only (no `catalog-names.ts` references remain).
- `pnpm --filter @repo/web typecheck` is green.

Testing: component test asserting that the fetch URL includes `?vertical=accommodation` and that items display their i18n label.

Switch label resolution to amenityNames i18n namespace
Update accommodation features component equivalently
Add component test for vertical param and i18n label rendering

tasks.title: Update commerce editor (AmenitiesFeaturesField.tsx) to use vertical-filtered catalog + i18n labels
tasks.description:
Update the commerce editor's amenities/features field to pass the correct vertical when fetching the catalog, and display labels from `@repo/i18n`.

The commerce editor is at `apps/web/src/components/commerce/AmenitiesFeaturesField.tsx`. It is used for BOTH gastronomy and experience listings. It must know which vertical is being edited so it can pass `?vertical=gastronomy` or `?vertical=experience` to the catalog endpoint.

The vertical is already known from the route context: `apps/web/src/pages/[lang]/mi-cuenta/comercio/[vertical]/[id]/editar.astro` passes `[vertical]` as a param. Trace how `vertical` flows into the component (likely a prop). If it does not currently flow in as a prop, add it.

Files affected:
- `apps/web/src/components/commerce/AmenitiesFeaturesField.tsx` — update fetch to `?vertical={vertical}`; update label resolution to `amenityNames.<slug>` and `featureNames.<slug>` via `@repo/i18n`.
- `apps/web/src/pages/[lang]/mi-cuenta/comercio/[vertical]/[id]/editar.astro` — verify `vertical` prop is passed to the component.

Acceptance criteria:
- A gastronomy editor shows only `wifi`, `parking` (shared-core) + the 10 gastronomy amenities; NO accommodation-only items like `ropa_de_cama` or `cuna`.
- An experience editor shows only shared-core + the 8 experience amenities.
- Labels come from `amenityNames` / `featureNames` i18n namespaces; no raw slug or null is rendered.
- `pnpm --filter @repo/web typecheck` is green.

Testing: component tests for gastronomy vertical (catalog filtered) and experience vertical (catalog filtered), verifying excluded accommodation-only slugs are absent.

tasks.title: Update admin amenity/feature form and list columns to handle applicableVerticals
tasks.description:
The admin panel has forms and list columns for managing catalog items. These must be updated to:
1. Show/edit `applicableVerticals` (a multi-select of `['accommodation','gastronomy','experience']`) in the amenity and feature create/edit forms.
2. Remove the `name` column from admin table columns (since `name` is dropped from the DB; display is now by slug via i18n lookup).
3. Replace any `name`-based display in admin grids with the slug (which is the i18n key) — the admin can show the slug in parentheses or use the i18n key to display the translated label via `translateAdminApiError` or the i18n hook.

Files affected (trace the exact paths in `apps/admin/src/`):
- Amenity list route / columns file (likely `apps/admin/src/routes/_authed/amenities/` or similar)
- Feature list route / columns file
- Amenity create/edit form
- Feature create/edit form

Acceptance criteria:
- Admin amenity/feature list shows `applicableVerticals` as a column.
- Admin create/edit form includes a multi-select for `applicableVerticals` with all 3 options.
- The `name` column no longer appears (it doesn't exist in the schema).
- `pnpm --filter @repo/admin typecheck` is green.

Testing: component tests for the form and list asserting `applicableVerticals` appears and `name` does not.

tasks.title: Update public accommodation/commerce detail pages to use featureNames for feature display
tasks.description:
Audit all public-facing pages that display amenity or feature labels to ensure they use the i18n namespaces (not the deleted `catalog-names.ts` or the dropped `name` column).

Priority surfaces:
1. Accommodation detail page (the amenity/feature chip lists visible to tourists).
2. Gastronomy listing detail page (if it renders amenity/feature chips).
3. Experience listing detail page (if it renders amenity/feature chips).

Search for usages of `amenityNames`, `featureNames`, and any remaining references to `.name` on amenity/feature objects from the API.

Files affected (trace actual paths):
- `apps/web/src/components/accommodation/AmenitiesSection.astro` or similar chip component
- Commerce detail page equivalents in `apps/web/src/pages/[lang]/`

Note: the API no longer returns a `name` field. Any component that accesses `amenity.name` or `feature.name` will silently render `undefined` unless fixed. This task is the audit + fix for display-side usage.

Acceptance criteria:
- Accommodation, gastronomy, and experience detail pages display amenity/feature labels in the user's locale.
- No `amenity.name` or `feature.name` property access remains in web components (replaced by i18n lookup on slug).
- `pnpm --filter @repo/web typecheck` green.

Testing: spot-check component tests for one accommodation and one commerce detail page asserting the slug-based label renders.

tasks.title: Integration test: data-migration safety — existing accommodation relation rows preserved
tasks.description:
Verify the hard constraint from §5.6 and §7: no existing accommodation listing's selected amenities or features must be lost by the migration.

This is an automated guard, not a manual check:

1. **Before-migration snapshot**: write a test that, in a test DB, inserts representative `r_accommodation_amenity` and `r_accommodation_feature` rows referencing existing amenity/feature catalog entries.
2. **Run migration**: apply the T-001 migration (`pnpm db:migrate` on the test DB).
3. **After-migration assertion**: assert that all pre-migration `r_accommodation_amenity.amenity_id` values still exist in `amenities.id` and that `applicable_verticals` on those rows contains `'accommodation'`. Same for features.
4. **Negative assertion**: assert that no `r_accommodation_amenity` or `r_accommodation_feature` rows were deleted by the migration.

The test should run against a real Postgres test DB (not a mock), matching the project's existing integration test pattern for DB tests.

Files affected:
- `packages/db/test/migrations/` (new test file) or wherever DB integration tests live — check the existing pattern.

Acceptance criteria:
- Test is added to the `@repo/db` test suite.
- `pnpm --filter @repo/db test` is green including this new test.
- Test explicitly counts relation rows before and after and asserts they are equal.

subtasks.title: Seed test DB with representative r_accommodation_amenity and r_accommodation_feature rows
Apply T-001 migration and assert relation rows are preserved
Assert applicable_verticals contains accommodation for all existing rows

tasks.title: End-to-end smoke: gastronomy editor shows only correct vertical catalog
tasks.description:
Manual + automated smoke verification that the full stack is working correctly after all implementation tasks complete:

**Automated checks (add to `apps/e2e` or as API-level HTTP test):**
1. `GET /api/v1/public/amenities?vertical=gastronomy` — response contains `delivery`, `wifi` (shared-core); does NOT contain `ropa_de_cama` (accommodation-only).
2. `GET /api/v1/public/features?vertical=experience` — response contains `kid_friendly`, `wifi` (shared-core); does NOT contain `pileta_climatizada` (accommodation-only).
3. `GET /api/v1/public/amenities` — response does NOT contain a `name` field on any item (dropped column).

**Manual smoke (document results in progress notes):**
1. Open the gastronomy editor for a test listing — verify the amenity/feature panel shows only gastronomy + shared-core items and labels are in Spanish.
2. Open the accommodation editor — verify the set is unchanged from before (no regression).
3. Open the experience editor — verify only experience + shared-core items appear.

Acceptance criteria:
- All 3 automated API assertions pass.
- Manual smoke passes (no accommodation-only items in commerce editors).
- No `name` field appears in any catalog endpoint response.

subtasks.title: Add HTTP-level tests for vertical filter + name field absence

tasks.title: Update CLAUDE.md notes and archive BETA-90 from backlog
tasks.description:
Minimal docs-and-cleanup task to close out the spec:

1. **Update root `CLAUDE.md`** — add a note in the 'Common Gotchas' or 'Patterns' section about:
   - The amenity/feature slug regex now allows underscores (as of this spec).
   - Display labels come from `amenityNames.<slug>` / `featureNames.<slug>` in `@repo/i18n` (not a `name` column).
   - Catalog endpoints accept `?vertical=` to scope results.

2. **Update `packages/seed/CLAUDE.md`** (or wherever seed docs live) to reference the new `applicableVerticals` field and the §10 taxonomy.

3. **Note BETA-90 as absorbed** — add a comment/note in the CLAUDE.md or spec that BETA-90 is handled by this spec so future sessions don't plan it again.

Files affected:
- `/home/qazuor/projects/WEBS/hospeda-SPEC-266-amenities-feature-catalog-by-vertical/CLAUDE.md` (worktree root)
- `packages/seed/CLAUDE.md`

Acceptance criteria:
- The CLAUDE.md mentions the slug regex change and the i18n display resolution pattern.
- `grep -r 'featureNames' CLAUDE.md` returns a result.
- No test failures from docs changes.
